note: Single aggregated file. Each case carries a `skill_name` so the skill-creator runner can route per case. See evals/README.md for the file-layout choice and the per-skill files under evals/<skill>/evals.json (kept in sync; this aggregated file is authoritative).

evals.name: sv-typography-mechanics
evals.expected_output: Corrected text with Swedish typography conventions applied; no style or word-order changes.
expectations: Thousands separator uses thin no-break space (U+202F), not comma: 1 234 567.
Percent in figures uses no-break space and `procent` is written out in running text per Swedish mechanics: 14 procent.
Range uses EN-dash without spaces (2–5), not hyphen with spaces (2 - 5).
Time uses full stop (14.30), not colon.
Temperature uses no-break space between number and degree sign (25 °C).

evals.name: sv-genitive-on-s-names
evals.expected_output: Apostrophe-s and apostrophe-only forms removed from names ending in -s; zero-marking is the first choice.
expectations: *Marx's teorier* is corrected to *Marx teorier* (zero-marking).
*CoMaps's föregångare* is corrected to *CoMaps föregångare* (zero-marking).
*Andreas's bok* is corrected to *Andreas bok* (zero-marking), not *Andreas's* and not *Andreas's bok*.
The apostrophe-s form is identified as an English interference pattern that is never correct in Swedish.

evals.name: sv-quotation-marks-and-dashes
evals.expected_output: Quotation marks normalised to ”…” (U+201D both sides); EN-dash with spaces, never EM-dash.
expectations: Outer quotation marks use ”…” (U+201D on both sides), not "…" straight ASCII or curly “…”.
Punctuation placement follows Swedish convention: commas/full stops inside the quote only when they belong to the quoted material.
The EN-dash with spaces is used for parenthetical insertions, never the EM-dash (—).

evals.name: sv-grammar-de-dem-and-compounds
evals.expected_output: De/dem corrected per subject/object; split-compounds (särskrivning) joined into single words.
expectations: *dom* is corrected to *de* (subject) or *dem* (object) per its syntactic role.
*sjukvårds personal* is joined into *sjukvårdspersonal* (single compound).
*sjuk husen* is joined into *sjukhusen*.
*schema läggning* is joined into *schemaläggning*.

`## Language resolution` in the transcript names `lib/languages/sv.md` as the loaded language file and identifies the *Mechanics* section as the applied scope (proofread is Phase-1-only). The without-skill baseline does not produce this scope record — the skill's value is that the loaded rule set is documented, not just that the right corrections happen to be made.

evals.name: sv-particskrivning-broad
evals.expected_output: All split-compound errors corrected to single compound words.
expectations: *sjukvårds anställda* → *sjukvårdsanställda*.
*sjuk sköterska* → *sjuksköterska*; *under sköterska* → *undersköterska*.
*brand kåren* → *brandkåren*; *brand män* → *brandmän*.
No style or word-order changes are made — proofread scope only.

`## Language resolution` in the transcript names `lib/languages/sv.md` as the loaded language file and identifies the *Mechanics* section as the applied scope (proofread is Phase-1-only). The without-skill baseline does not produce this scope record — the skill's value is that the loaded rule set is documented, not just that the right corrections happen to be made.

evals.name: sv-greetings-and-closings
evals.expected_output: Greeting uses ! not ,; closing salutation has no comma before the name.
expectations: *Hej Susanne,* is corrected to *Hej Susanne!* (exclamation mark, not comma).
*Med vänlig hälsning,* loses the trailing comma — the closing phrase stands on its own line.

evals.name: en_GB-typography-mechanics
evals.expected_output: Corrected text with British English typography conventions applied.
expectations: Thousands separator uses comma: 1,234,567.
Percent in figures uses space: 14 %.
Parenthetical insertion uses EN-dash with spaces (an increase – of …), never EM-dash without spaces.
Date format is DD/MM/YYYY (04/07/2026), not MM/DD/YYYY.
Time format is 24-hour with full stop (14.30), not 12-hour with AM/PM.

evals.name: en_GB-oxford-comma-omitted
evals.expected_output: Oxford comma is removed in series; British convention favours no Oxford comma.
expectations: *red, white, and blue* is corrected to *red, white and blue* (no Oxford comma).
No other word-order or content changes are made — proofread scope only.

evals.name: en_GB-quotation-marks-and-punctuation
evals.expected_output: Single curly quotes outer, double curly inner; punctuation outside the quote unless part of quoted material.
expectations: Outer quotation marks use ‘…’ (U+2018, U+2019), not "…" straight or “…” double.
Inner quotation marks use “…”, opposite of American convention.
Commas and full stops sit outside the closing quote unless they are part of the quoted material — *He called it ‘revolutionary’.* not *‘revolutionary,’*.

evals.name: en_GB-abbreviation-fullstops
evals.expected_output: Contraction abbreviations (Mr, Dr) carry no full stop; truncations (etc., vol.) do.
expectations: *Mr. Smith* is corrected to *Mr Smith* (contraction — no full stop).
*Dr. Jones* is corrected to *Dr Jones*.
Initialisms (GDP, NATO, BBC) carry no full stops.

evals.name: en_GB-ise-isation-spelling
evals.expected_output: American -ize/-ization spellings corrected to British -ise/-isation; American color/center to British colour/centre; American traveler to traveller; American favorite to favourite.
expectations: *organize* → *organise*, *analyze* → *analyse*, *finalize* → *finalise*.
*color* → *colour*; *favorite* → *favourite*; *center* → *centre*.
*traveler* → *traveller* (double-l).
*on accident* → *by accident*.

`## Language resolution` in the transcript names `lib/languages/en_GB.md` as the loaded language file and identifies the *Mechanics* section as the applied scope (proofread is Phase-1-only). The without-skill baseline does not produce this scope record — the skill's value is that the loaded rule set is documented, not just that the right corrections happen to be made.

evals.name: en_GB-currency-and-temperature
evals.expected_output: Currency before number with no space; temperature with no-break space between number and degree.
expectations: Currency uses *£100* (no space, symbol before).
Temperature uses no-break space: *25 °C* not *25°C*.
Decimal separator is the full stop (123.45).
Thousands separator is the comma (1,234,567).

`## Language resolution` in the transcript names `lib/languages/en_GB.md` as the loaded language file and identifies the *Mechanics* section as the applied scope (proofread is Phase-1-only). The without-skill baseline does not produce this scope record — the skill's value is that the loaded rule set is documented, not just that the right corrections happen to be made.

expectations: *organise* → *organize*; *analyse* → *analyze*.

evals.name: sv-negative-clean-text
evals.expected_output: Input already follows Swedish mechanics conventions. Proofread delivers the text unchanged (or with at most a trivial change) and does not introduce false-positive 'corrections' such as removing the thin no-break space, converting curly quotes, or rewriting the time format.
expectations: The output text is unchanged from the input, OR any change is limited to whitespace-equivalent normalisation (no semantic edits).
The thin no-break space (U+202F) inside `1 234 567` is preserved — not normalised to a regular space and not replaced by a comma or full stop.
Swedish quotation marks ”…” (U+201D both sides) are preserved — not converted to straight ASCII "…" and not to curly “…”.
Time `kl. 10.30` is preserved with a full stop — not changed to `10:30`.
Genitive `Marx teorier` (zero-marking on -s name) is preserved — no apostrophe-s is inserted.
`## Language resolution` in the transcript names `lib/languages/sv.md` and identifies the *Mechanics* section as the applied scope; the without-skill baseline lacks this record.

evals.name: en_GB-negative-clean-text
evals.expected_output: Input already follows British English mechanics. Proofread preserves -ise/-our spellings, omits the Oxford comma in the list, and does not Americanise the text.
expectations: The output text is unchanged from the input, OR any change is limited to whitespace-equivalent normalisation.
British spellings *analyse*, *refurbishment*, *metres* are preserved — not changed to *analyze*, *remodeling*, *meters*.
The series `readings, music and short workshops` is preserved without an Oxford comma (British convention).
Single quotation marks `'…'` around the librarian quote are preserved — not converted to double quotes.
The abbreviation `Department for Transport` and its currency format `£1,234,567` are preserved — not Americanised.
`## Language resolution` names `lib/languages/en_GB.md` and identifies the *Mechanics* section as the applied scope; the without-skill baseline lacks this record.

evals.name: en_US-negative-clean-text
evals.expected_output: Input already follows American English mechanics. Proofread preserves -ize/-or spellings, keeps the Oxford comma, and does not Britishise the text.
expectations: The output text is unchanged from the input, OR any change is limited to whitespace-equivalent normalisation.
American spellings *analyze*, *renovation*, *feet* are preserved — not changed to British equivalents.
The series `readings, music, and short workshops` is preserved with the Oxford comma (American convention).
Double quotation marks `"…"` around the librarian quote are preserved — not converted to single quotes.
Time format `10:30 a.m.` is preserved with the colon and the lowercase periods (American convention).
`## Language resolution` names `lib/languages/en_US.md` and identifies the *Mechanics* section as the applied scope; the without-skill baseline lacks this record.

evals.name: sv-redline-typography-and-style
evals.expected_output: Finding list covering both mechanics and style for a Swedish article.
expectations: Phase 1 (proofread) applies before Phase 2 — the corrected text flows into the critical review, not delivered separately.
Phase 2 surfaces findings against `rules/style.md` and the Style section of `sv.md`.
Phase 3 settles each finding one at a time via the dialogue protocol (accept / reject / counter / delegate).
Genre resolves to `article` via either the standard flow or fast-path; the H1 + standfirst + byline force the standard flow.

evals.name: sv-redline-genitive-on-s
evals.expected_output: Mechanics finding: *Marx teorier* and *CoMaps föregångare* — explicitly flagged as correct zero-marking; any apostrophe-s drift would be a finding.
expectations: *Marx teorier* in the article body is recognised as correct Swedish zero-marking for an -s name.
*CoMaps föregångare* is recognised as correct.
If any apostrophe-s form appears in the redline-corrected output, that is a regression and must be flagged.

evals.name: sv-redline-press-release-attribution
evals.expected_output: Findings flag that the press release uses straight ASCII quotation marks; speech dashes are the Swedish press-release standard for attribution.
expectations: Straight ASCII quotation marks are flagged or corrected to ”…” (Swedish outer).
A finding raises that speech dashes (talstreck — EN-dash with space) are the Swedish convention for attributed speech in press releases.
Genre resolves to `press-release` via the prompt trigger or the structural markers.

evals.name: sv-redline-ai-tells
evals.expected_output: Style-layer findings on AI-tell calques and anglicisms.
expectations: *Det är värt att notera att…* is flagged as an AI-tell calque of *It's worth noting that…*.
*Sammanfattningsvis* at the start of a conclusion is flagged as formulaic.
*I en värld där…* is flagged as a calque opening cliché.
*adressera*, *navigera*, *leverera* (metaphoric), *metrik*, *regulator-miljön* are flagged as anglicism interference and Swedish substitutes are proposed.

evals.name: sv-redline-overlong-heading
evals.expected_output: Findings raise H1 length and H2 length as structural issues.
expectations: The H1 (well over 60 characters) is flagged as too long for a readable headline.
At least one of the over-long H2 headings is flagged.
The finding is concrete (line-editing scope) — not punted to the developmental last-resort finding.

`## Genre / technique resolution` in the transcript names the committed genre and documents whether the fast-path exited or the standard flow read `lib/genres/_index.md` and the matching genre file. The without-skill baseline does not produce this resolution record — the skill's value is the explicit genre commitment that scopes the review pass.

evals.name: sv-redline-fabricated-source
evals.expected_output: Findings flag the suspicious-looking references (specific publishers, journal names, dates) so the writer can verify them.
expectations: At least one finding flags one or more of the source citations as unverifiable / likely fabricated, prompting the writer to verify.
The finding is framed as `verify` rather than asserting fabrication as fact — the redline pass surfaces, the writer confirms.
Quoted attribution that names a specific researcher is flagged as needing verification.

evals.name: en_GB-redline-typography-and-style
evals.expected_output: Finding list covering both mechanics and style for a British English article.
expectations: Phase 1 (proofread) is applied silently; corrected text flows into Phase 2.
Genre resolves to `article` (H1 + standfirst + byline force the standard flow).
Findings respect British conventions: single outer curly quotes, no Oxford comma, DD/MM/YYYY dates, EN-dash with spaces for parenthetical insertion.

evals.name: en_GB-redline-quotation-attribution
evals.expected_output: Findings on quotation-mark typography and punctuation placement.
expectations: Outer single curly quotes are used; inner double curly quotes are used.
Punctuation sits outside the closing quote unless it belongs to the quoted material (logical/British convention).
Question marks belonging to the spoken utterance stay inside the quotation.

evals.name: en_GB-redline-ai-tells
evals.expected_output: Style-layer findings on AI-tell vocabulary and openings.
expectations: *In a world where…* is flagged as an opening cliché.
*It's worth noting that…* and *Let me be perfectly clear* are flagged as AI-tell constructions.
*delve into*, *leverage* (as a verb), *multifaceted*, *tapestry*, *robust*, *comprehensive*, *ultimately*, *streamline* are flagged as AI vocabulary tells.
*Furthermore* used as a crutch between paragraphs is flagged as a symptom of weak structure.

evals.name: en_GB-redline-american-drift
evals.expected_output: Findings flag American spellings, American idioms, and American date drift.
expectations: American -ize/-ization spellings flagged for the British -ise/-isation substitute.
*Going forward* flagged for the British substitute *in future* or omission.
*Reach out to* flagged for *contact* or *get in touch with*.
*Leverage* as a verb flagged for *use*, *exploit*, *draw on*.

evals.name: en_GB-redline-overlong-heading
evals.expected_output: Findings raise heading length even when the source is Swedish (auto-detection should pick sv; with explicit en_GB the pass still flags structural issues).
expectations: Headings over 60 characters are flagged as too long for a readable headline.
The finding is concrete and falls inside line-editing scope (not punted to developmental).
Genre resolves to `article` via the structural markers.

evals.name: en_GB-redline-fast-path-short-text
evals.expected_output: Fast-path commits to the `general` genre because the text has no structural markers and the prompt has no genre word.
expectations: The fast-path exits before reading `lib/genres/_index.md`.
Genre commits to `general` (the genre with `default: true`).
The default technique is `none`, so no technique file is loaded.
Finding list (if any) is short and reflects the everyday-text register.

evals.name: en_US-redline-typography-and-style
evals.expected_output: Finding list covering both mechanics and style for an American English article.
expectations: Genre resolves to `article` via standard flow (H1 + standfirst + byline present).
American conventions are applied in findings: double outer curly quotes, Oxford comma in series, MM/DD/YYYY dates, EM-dash without spaces for parenthetical insertion.
Phase 1 (proofread) is applied silently; corrected text flows into Phase 2.
Phase 3 settles each finding one at a time via the dialogue protocol.

evals.name: en_US-redline-quotation-and-punctuation
evals.expected_output: Findings on quotation-mark typography and the American inside-the-quote punctuation convention.
expectations: Outer quotation marks should be "…" (double curly), inner '…' (single curly) — opposite of British.
Commas and full stops always go inside the closing quote — *He called it "revolutionary."* — regardless of whether the punctuation belongs to the quote.
Question marks belonging to the spoken utterance stay inside the quotation.

`## Genre / technique resolution` in the transcript names the committed genre and documents whether the fast-path exited or the standard flow read `lib/genres/_index.md` and the matching genre file. The without-skill baseline does not produce this resolution record — the skill's value is the explicit genre commitment that scopes the review pass.

evals.name: en_US-redline-ai-tells
evals.expected_output: Style-layer findings on AI-tell vocabulary and openings, including American-specific tells.
expectations: *In a world where…*, *It's worth noting that…*, *Let me be perfectly clear* are flagged.
*delve into*, *leverage* (as a verb), *multifaceted*, *tapestry*, *robust*, *comprehensive*, *ultimately*, *unprecedented*, *streamline* are flagged as AI vocabulary tells.
American-specific AI tell *game-changer* is flagged.
American-specific AI tell *paradigm shift* is flagged.
American-specific AI tell *next-generation* is flagged.
Management jargon *circle back* is flagged.
Management jargon *touch base* is flagged.
Management jargon *holistic approach* is flagged.
Management jargon *strategic* is flagged.
Management jargon *proactive* is flagged.
Management jargon *synergies* is flagged.

`## Genre / technique resolution` in the transcript names the committed genre and documents whether the fast-path exited or the standard flow read `lib/genres/_index.md` and the matching genre file. The without-skill baseline does not produce this resolution record — the skill's value is the explicit genre commitment that scopes the review pass.

evals.name: en_US-redline-oxford-comma-and-em-dash
evals.expected_output: Findings raise the missing Oxford comma and the wrong parenthetical dash form.
expectations: *red, white and blue* is flagged — the Oxford comma is required in American English: *red, white, and blue*.
Parenthetical insertion uses EM-dash without spaces (*market—an increase*), not EN-dash with spaces (*market – an increase*).
Time format is 12-hour with AM/PM and colon (2:30 PM), not 24-hour with full stop.

`## Genre / technique resolution` in the transcript names the committed genre and documents whether the fast-path exited or the standard flow read `lib/genres/_index.md` and the matching genre file. The without-skill baseline does not produce this resolution record — the skill's value is the explicit genre commitment that scopes the review pass.

evals.name: en_US-redline-fast-path-short-text
evals.expected_output: Fast-path commits to the `general` genre because the text has no structural markers and the prompt has no genre word.
expectations: The fast-path exits before reading `lib/genres/_index.md`.
Genre commits to `general` (the genre with `default: true`).
The default technique is `none`, so no technique file is loaded.
Findings (if any) are short and reflect everyday-text register.

evals.name: en_US-redline-abbreviation-fullstops
evals.expected_output: Findings flag missing full stops on contraction abbreviations.
expectations: *Mr Smith* and *Dr Jones* are flagged — American English always uses the full stop: *Mr. Smith*, *Dr. Jones*.
Initialisms (CEO, HTML, NASA) carry no full stops.
British -ise/-isation drift (*organise*, *analyse*) is flagged for the American substitute.

`## Genre / technique resolution` in the transcript names the committed genre and documents whether the fast-path exited or the standard flow read `lib/genres/_index.md` and the matching genre file. The without-skill baseline does not produce this resolution record — the skill's value is the explicit genre commitment that scopes the review pass.

evals.name: sv-negative-clean-press-release
evals.expected_output: A clean Swedish press release with proper genre conventions. Redline produces zero or near-zero findings. The dialogue phase is short or noted as not entered. No false-positive style findings are raised.
expectations: `## Findings` is empty or contains at most one minor finding (no wall of false positives).
If any finding is raised, the transcript justifies it with a concrete citation from a loaded rule or genre file — no `## Findings` items are added on vibes.
`## Genre / technique resolution` commits to `press-release` (or the closest matching genre) based on the structural markers — not to the default `general` fallback.
Output is identical or near-identical to the input — no spurious style rewrites of clean sentences.
`## Phase log` records Phase 3 entered briefly (or skipped if no findings); the dialogue is not artificially inflated to demonstrate the protocol on a clean text.

evals.name: sv-edit-article-polish
evals.expected_output: Polished Swedish article — mechanics and style applied directly, no user dialogue.
expectations: The main agent settles the finding list directly without invoking a subagent (default `--max-iterations=0`).
Genre resolves to `article` via standard flow (H1 + standfirst + byline present).
Mechanics (Phase 1) and style (Phase 2) findings are both applied to the polished output.
*Marx teorier* and *CoMaps föregångare* retain the correct zero-marking after polishing.

evals.name: sv-edit-press-release-attribution
evals.expected_output: Polished Swedish press release with speech-dash attribution and Swedish outer quotation marks.
expectations: Attributed speech is converted to speech-dash form (talstreck — EN-dash with space) or, where verbatim wording matters, kept in ”…” marks.
Quotation marks are normalised to ”…” (Swedish outer).
Genre resolves to `press-release` via the prompt trigger.
The polished text preserves the news content and the attributed source.

evals.name: sv-edit-with-max-iterations-deep-natural-language
evals.prompt: /edit sv kör djupt på den här texten
evals.expected_output: The natural-language phrase *kör djupt* maps to --max-iterations=3 and the subagent loop is invoked.
expectations: The subagent loop in `lib/protocols/subagent.md` is invoked with a ceiling of 3 iterations.
The natural-language phrase *kör djupt* is parsed to 3 — the same value as the `--max-iterations=3` flag.
The subagent's convergence rules still apply — early consensus stops the loop before 3 rounds if main and subagent agree.
No user dialogue happens in Phase 3 — `/edit` is the AFK variant.

evals.name: sv-edit-ai-tells-and-anglicisms-applied
evals.expected_output: Polished output replaces AI-tells and anglicisms with Swedish substitutes.
expectations: *Det är värt att notera att…*, *I en värld där…*, *Sammanfattningsvis*, *Dyk djupare* are removed or rewritten.

*adressera* becomes *ta upp* or *behandla*; *navigera* becomes *hantera*; *leverera* becomes *ge* or *erbjuda*; *metrik* becomes *mätvärde* or *nyckeltal*; *regulatorer* becomes *myndigheter* or *tillsynsmyndigheter*.
Split-compound errors are corrected as part of Phase 1.

evals.name: sv-edit-overlong-heading
evals.expected_output: Polished version shortens over-long headings while preserving meaning.
expectations: The H1 is shortened to a readable length (under 60 characters where the meaning allows).
At least one over-long H2 is shortened.
The body text under each heading remains substantively unchanged — only headings and obviously broken sentences are touched.

`## Genre / technique resolution` records both the committed genre and the committed technique, and `## Phase log` includes a Phase 2 entry citing `lib/rules/style.md` as the applied style foundation. The without-skill baseline does not produce these structured records — the skill's value is the protocol fidelity behind the corrections, not the corrections alone.

evals.name: sv-edit-fabricated-source-flagged-as-closing-note
evals.expected_output: Polished text retains structure; suspicious source citations are flagged as a closing note for the writer to verify.
expectations: Cited sources that look fabricated are preserved verbatim in the polished output (`/edit` does not invent or correct facts).
A closing note alerts the writer that one or more sources need verification, naming the specific lines or citations.
Attributed quotes preserve their attribution (the named researcher's name is not changed).

evals.name: en_GB-edit-article-polish
evals.expected_output: Polished British English article with mechanics and style applied directly.
expectations: Genre resolves to `article` via standard flow.
British conventions are applied: single outer curly quotes, no Oxford comma, DD/MM/YYYY dates, EN-dash with spaces for parenthetical insertion.
Attribution uses single curly quotes (British convention), not speech dashes (which are Swedish).
The main agent settles the findings directly without invoking a subagent.

evals.name: en_GB-edit-style-cleanup
evals.expected_output: Polished output strips AI-tell vocabulary and tightens the prose.
expectations: *In a world where…*, *it's worth noting that*, *Let me be perfectly clear* are removed.
*delve into*, *leverage* (verb), *multifaceted*, *tapestry*, *robust*, *comprehensive*, *ultimately*, *streamline* are replaced or removed.
*Furthermore*/*moreover* as paragraph crutches are removed; paragraph structure is tightened.
*To summarize* opening a conclusion is rewritten.

evals.name: en_GB-edit-with-max-iterations-flag
evals.expected_output: Subagent loop runs with a ceiling of 2 iterations.
expectations: The `--max-iterations=2` flag is honoured.
The subagent loop in `lib/protocols/subagent.md` is invoked with a ceiling of 2.
The subagent's convergence rules still apply — early consensus stops the loop before 2 rounds if main and subagent agree.

evals.name: en_GB-edit-american-drift-corrected
evals.expected_output: Polished British English with -ise/-isation spellings, BrE idioms, and BrE date order.
expectations: *organize* → *organise*; *analyze* → *analyse*; *finalize* → *finalise*.
*color* → *colour*; *favorite* → *favourite*; *center* → *centre*; *traveler* → *traveller*.
*Going forward* → *in future* or omitted.
*Leverage* (verb) → *use*, *exploit*, or *draw on*.

evals.name: en_GB-edit-overlong-heading
evals.expected_output: Headings shortened; body preserved.
expectations: The H1 is shortened to a readable length.
Body text under each heading remains substantively unchanged.

`## Genre / technique resolution` records both the committed genre and the committed technique, and `## Phase log` includes a Phase 2 entry citing `lib/rules/style.md` as the applied style foundation. The without-skill baseline does not produce these structured records — the skill's value is the protocol fidelity behind the corrections, not the corrections alone.

evals.name: en_GB-edit-fabricated-source-preserved-with-note
evals.expected_output: Polished text preserves source citations verbatim; a closing note asks the writer to verify the citations.
expectations: Cited sources are preserved verbatim in the polished output.
A closing note flags the specific citations as needing verification.
Attributed quotes preserve attribution (researcher's name and quote stay together).

evals.name: en_US-edit-article-polish
evals.expected_output: Polished American English article with mechanics and style applied directly.
expectations: Genre resolves to `article` via standard flow.
American conventions are applied: double outer curly quotes, Oxford comma in series, MM/DD/YYYY dates, EM-dash without spaces for parenthetical insertion.
Attribution uses double curly quotes (American convention), not speech dashes.
The main agent settles the findings directly without invoking a subagent (default `--max-iterations=0`).

evals.name: en_US-edit-style-cleanup
evals.expected_output: Polished output strips AI-tell vocabulary and tightens the prose.
expectations: *In a world where…*, *it's worth noting that*, *Let me be perfectly clear* are removed.
*delve into*, *leverage* (verb), *multifaceted*, *tapestry*, *robust*, *comprehensive*, *ultimately*, *unprecedented*, *streamline* are replaced or removed.
*game-changer*, *paradigm shift*, *holistic approach*, *circle back* are removed or replaced.
Polished output uses Oxford comma in series.

`## Genre / technique resolution` records both the committed genre and the committed technique, and `## Phase log` includes a Phase 2 entry citing `lib/rules/style.md` as the applied style foundation. The without-skill baseline does not produce these structured records — the skill's value is the protocol fidelity behind the corrections, not the corrections alone.

evals.name: en_US-edit-quotation-applied
evals.expected_output: Polished version normalises to American conventions for quotation marks and punctuation placement.
expectations: Outer quotation marks use "…" (double curly); inner '…' (single curly).
Commas and full stops are placed inside the closing quote regardless of whether they belong to the quoted material.
Question marks belonging to the spoken utterance stay inside the quotation.

`## Genre / technique resolution` records both the committed genre and the committed technique, and `## Phase log` includes a Phase 2 entry citing `lib/rules/style.md` as the applied style foundation. The without-skill baseline does not produce these structured records — the skill's value is the protocol fidelity behind the corrections, not the corrections alone.

evals.name: en_US-edit-with-max-iterations-flag-three
evals.expected_output: Subagent loop runs with a ceiling of 3 iterations.
expectations: The `--max-iterations=3` flag is honoured.
The subagent loop in `lib/protocols/subagent.md` is invoked with a ceiling of 3.
Early consensus stops the loop before 3 rounds if main and subagent agree.
Values above 3 would be clamped to 3 (protocol maximum).

evals.name: en_US-edit-typography-applied
evals.expected_output: Polished version applies American typography conventions throughout.
expectations: Thousands separator is the comma; percent in figures has no space.
Parenthetical insertion uses EM-dash without spaces.
Dates are MM/DD/YYYY; time is 12-hour with AM/PM and colon.
Oxford comma is present in series; abbreviations carry full stops (*Mr.*, *Dr.*).
British -ise/-isation drift is corrected to American -ize/-ization.

evals.name: en_US-edit-overlong-heading
evals.expected_output: Headings shortened; body preserved.
expectations: The H1 is shortened to a readable length.
At least one over-long H2 is shortened.
Body text under each heading remains substantively unchanged.

`## Genre / technique resolution` records both the committed genre and the committed technique, and `## Phase log` includes a Phase 2 entry citing `lib/rules/style.md` as the applied style foundation. The without-skill baseline does not produce these structured records — the skill's value is the protocol fidelity behind the corrections, not the corrections alone.

evals.name: en_GB-negative-clean-essay
evals.expected_output: A clean British English essay-style text. Edit produces a polished version with minimal substantive changes; protocol fidelity is the testable signal, not the volume of edits.
expectations: `## Findings` raises at most a small number of substantive items (no wall of false positives on clean prose).
British conventions are preserved: -ise spellings, no Oxford comma in standard series, single quotation marks, £ currency, metric units with no-break space (`21 °C`, `320 square metres`).
`## Genre / technique resolution` commits to the closest matching genre — `column` or `general` — and names a technique if the genre's `default_technique` is not `none`.
`## Phase log` includes a Phase 2 entry citing `lib/rules/style.md` as the applied style foundation.
Any change applied in the edit is traceable to a finding in the transcript — no silent rewrites.

evals.name: sv-write-article-brief-flow
evals.prompt: /write skriv en artikel om hur fjärrvärme i Sverige förändras de kommande fem åren — kör på utan att fråga om varje fält
evals.expected_output: A Swedish article draft polished by Phase 4 (redline + subagent), preceded by the four-phase flow.
expectations: Phase 1 proposes values for all nine briefing fields (working title, target audience, sender purpose, reader takeaway, angle, hook, channel, length, content type) — no field is silently filled.
Phase 2 presents an idea (structure, tone, ABT or PAC plan, address, approximate length); bypass behaviour skips only the wait, not the idea.
Phase 3 produces the draft per `article` (default_technique `abt`).
Phase 4 polishes the draft and the final text follows Swedish mechanics and style — *14 procent*, ”…” quotation marks, EN-dash with spaces, no anglicisms, no AI-tell calques.

evals.name: sv-write-press-release
evals.prompt: /write skriv ett pressmeddelande från Kntnt om version 0.4.0 av textgranskningspluginet — accept all, kör på utan att vänta
evals.expected_output: A Swedish press release polished by Phase 4 with speech-dash attribution.
expectations: Genre is committed to `press-release` (default_technique `none` — no technique file loaded).
Phase 4 applies Swedish mechanics: speech dashes for attribution, no *du* address, no first-person sender voice.
Final text shows no anglicism interference and no AI-tell openings.
*procent* is written out in running text; numbers and units carry no-break space.

evals.name: sv-write-with-max-iterations-deep
evals.prompt: /write skriv en artikel om hur AI förändrar svensk journalistik — accept all, kör djupt på Phase 4
evals.expected_output: Phase 4 settles via the subagent loop with a ceiling of 3 (natural-language *kör djupt* → 3).
expectations: Phase 4 invokes the subagent loop in `lib/protocols/subagent.md` with a ceiling of 3 iterations.
The natural-language phrase *kör djupt* is parsed to 3 — the same value as the `--max-iterations=3` flag.
The subagent's convergence rules still apply — early consensus stops the loop before 3 rounds.
There is no post-draft user-facing dialogue beyond the optional last-resort closing note.

evals.name: sv-write-report-pac-technique
evals.prompt: /write skriv en rapport om svenska kommuners digitaliseringsstatus — accept all, kör på utan att fråga
evals.expected_output: A Swedish report draft using the PAC technique (the default for `report`).
expectations: Genre is committed to `report` (default_technique `pac`).
The PAC technique file is loaded and applied in Phase 3.
Phase 4 polish follows Swedish report conventions — no *du*, *procent* written out, ISO date format or *den 24 mars*.
Address is third-person; *vi* refers to the reporting organisation only where natural.

evals.name: sv-write-teaser
evals.prompt: /write skriv en puff för artikeln om cykelnätet (länk till artikelutkastet finns i förra svaret) — accept all
evals.expected_output: A short Swedish teaser (puff) using the ABT technique.
expectations: Genre is committed to `teaser` (the Swedish term *puff* is in the trigger list).
Phase 4 polish respects Swedish teaser conventions — short, single-paragraph, with a clear hook.
No AI-tell openings (*I en värld där…*, *Sammanfattningsvis*).
The teaser refers to the article without copy-pasting its standfirst.

evals.name: sv-write-no-technique-override
evals.prompt: /write skriv en kort artikel om cykelnätet utan ABT, no technique — accept all
evals.expected_output: Article drafted without applying ABT or any other technique, per the user's override.
expectations: The technique override is honoured — no technique file is loaded.
Genre is still committed to `article`.
Phase 4 polish applies Swedish mechanics and style without any technique-specific structural requirements.

evals.name: en_GB-write-article
evals.prompt: /write write an article in British English about how AI is changing UK newsrooms — accept all, just produce the draft
evals.expected_output: A British English article with mechanics and style applied through Phase 4.
expectations: Language resolves to en_GB via propose mode with the user's explicit *British English* override.
Genre resolves to `article` (default_technique `abt`).
Phase 4 enforces British conventions: single outer curly quotes, no Oxford comma, DD/MM/YYYY dates, EN-dash with spaces, -ise/-isation spellings.
No AI-tell vocabulary in the polished text (*delve*, *leverage*, *multifaceted*, *tapestry*).

evals.name: en_GB-write-with-max-iterations-flag
evals.prompt: /write --max-iterations=1 write an article in British English about urban cycling — accept all, no need to wait for approval
evals.expected_output: Phase 4 uses the subagent loop with a ceiling of 1.
expectations: The `--max-iterations=1` flag is honoured — one round of subagent settling.
Bypass behaviour skips the Phase 2 wait only; the idea is still presented.
Final output follows British English conventions.

evals.name: en_GB-write-press-release
evals.prompt: /write write a British English press release announcing version 0.4.0 of the Kntnt text plugin — accept all
evals.expected_output: British English press release with attribution in single curly quotes.
expectations: Genre commits to `press-release` (default_technique `none`).
Attribution uses single curly quotation marks ('…'), not speech dashes (which are Swedish).
Dates are DD/MM/YYYY.
No AI-tell openings.

evals.name: en_GB-write-column-personal-voice
evals.prompt: /write write a British English column about why I left Google Docs for plain Markdown — accept all, kör på
evals.expected_output: British English column with direct address and personal voice.
expectations: Genre commits to `column` (default_technique `abt`).
Address uses direct *you* and visible *I* where the column form invites it.
Free language is permitted — sentence fragments, conjunctions as openers where they serve the voice.
British conventions for spelling and punctuation are applied.

evals.name: en_GB-write-report-pac
evals.prompt: /write write a British English report on remote-work trends in UK SMEs — accept all
evals.expected_output: British English report drafted with PAC technique.
expectations: Genre commits to `report` (default_technique `pac`).
Technique file `pac.md` is loaded.
Address is third-person / *the reader* — no *you* in the report register.
British conventions throughout.

evals.name: en_GB-write-teaser
evals.prompt: /write write a British English teaser/standfirst for an article on urban cycling — accept all
evals.expected_output: A short British English teaser using the ABT technique.
expectations: Genre commits to `teaser`.
Output is short and self-contained.
British conventions for spelling and punctuation.
No AI-tell vocabulary.

evals.name: en_US-write-article
evals.prompt: /write write an article in American English about how AI is changing US local news — accept all, just produce the draft
evals.expected_output: An American English article with mechanics and style applied through Phase 4.
expectations: Language resolves to en_US.
Genre commits to `article` (default_technique `abt`).
Phase 4 enforces American conventions: double outer curly quotes, Oxford comma, MM/DD/YYYY dates, EM-dash without spaces for parenthetical insertion, -ize/-ization spellings.
No AI-tell vocabulary in the polished text.

evals.name: en_US-write-press-release
evals.prompt: /write write an American English press release announcing version 0.4.0 of the Kntnt text plugin — accept all
evals.expected_output: American English press release with attribution in double curly quotes.
expectations: Genre commits to `press-release`.
Attribution uses double curly quotation marks ("…"), not speech dashes.
Dates are MM/DD/YYYY.
Oxford comma in series.

evals.name: en_US-write-with-max-iterations-zero
evals.prompt: /write --max-iterations=0 write an American English short web copy block about the new product page — accept all, hoppa över subagent, just produce the draft
evals.expected_output: Phase 4 settles directly without a subagent (flag set to 0).
expectations: The `--max-iterations=0` flag is honoured — no subagent is invoked.
The natural-language phrase *hoppa över subagent* corroborates the flag.
The main agent applies the finding list directly and delivers the polished result.
American conventions are applied throughout.

evals.name: en_US-write-case-study
evals.prompt: /write write an American English case study about an SMB that migrated from Google Workspace to a self-hosted stack — accept all
evals.expected_output: American English case study where interviewees drive the narrative through attributed double-quote speech.
expectations: Genre commits to `case-study` (default_technique `abt`).
Address avoids *you*; the interviewees carry the narrative.
The sender is referred to in the third person — never as *we* or *our solution* in the writer's voice.
Attribution uses double curly quotes; Oxford comma in series.

evals.name: en_US-write-web-copy
evals.prompt: /write write American English web copy for the homepage hero of an indie tech site — accept all
evals.expected_output: Short American English web copy with hook and CTA.
expectations: Genre commits to `web-copy` (default_technique `abt`).
Output is short, scannable, and carries a clear hook plus a CTA tied to the sender purpose.
American spelling and punctuation throughout.
No AI-tell openings or vocabulary.

evals.name: en_US-write-opinion-piece
evals.prompt: /write write an American English op-ed arguing that subscription fatigue is killing creator economics — accept all
evals.expected_output: American English opinion piece with a clear stance and ABT structure.
expectations: Genre commits to `opinion` (default_technique `abt`).
The piece takes a clear stance — no false balance.
American conventions for spelling, punctuation, and date order.
Direct *you* where the form invites it; no AI-tell crutches.

evals.name: fallback-default-mechanics-on-german-text
evals.expected_output: The skill falls back to `default-mechanics.md` because there is no `de.md`, reports the absence, and applies the baseline conventions to obvious mechanic errors.
expectations: Language resolution detects German, finds no `lib/languages/de.md`, and falls back to `lib/languages/default-mechanics.md`.
The reply prints the fallback notice in English: *No language file found for [German]. Baseline conventions from `default-mechanics.md` apply. Add `lib/languages/<code>.md` for stricter control.*
Baseline conventions apply: ISO 8601 short date, SI no-break space between number and unit, comma decimal not used (full stop is the default-mechanics baseline).
The duplicated word *doppelte doppelte* is flagged; the typo *Tippfehlr* is corrected; the missing trailing word is flagged.

evals.name: overlay-loader-sv_FI-territorial-variant
evals.expected_output: The overlay loader applies `sv_FI.md` over `sv.md` per `lib/protocols/language-resolution.md`. The `Typography` H2 from the variant replaces the base; every other H2 under the layer markers is carried through from `sv.md` unchanged.
expectations: Language resolution loads `sv_FI.md` and applies the overlay over `sv.md` because the variant declares `inherits: "@sv.md"`.
The variant's `Typography` H2 replaces the base's `Typography` H2 wholesale — time becomes `14:30` (colon, Finnish convention), not `14.30` (full stop, Sweden-Swedish).
Other mechanics H2 sections (`Quotation marks`, `Quotation conventions`, `Punctuation conventions`, `Grammar specifics`, `Greetings and closings`) are carried through from `sv.md` unchanged.
All style H2 sections from `sv.md` are carried through unchanged (the variant has no style overrides).
Inheritance is one step only — the base `sv.md` does not itself inherit.

evals.name: fast-path-hit-short-unsignalled-sv-text
evals.expected_output: Fast-path commits to `general` and skips both the `_index.md` read and the matched-genre file read.
expectations: The fast-path exits before reading `lib/genres/_index.md` — the input has no H1, no standfirst, no byline, no attributed-quote pattern, and the prompt has no genre word.
Genre commits to `general` (the genre with `default: true`).
The default technique is `none`, so no technique file is loaded.

The fast-path output reflects the `general` genre fallback (`default_technique: none`); no behaviour beyond what the general genre prescribes is introduced — the fast-path is a dedup of the same downstream rules, not a behaviour change.

evals.name: fast-path-standard-flow-on-structured-article
evals.expected_output: The standard flow kicks in because the input carries an H1, a standfirst, a byline, and attributed-quote patterns — the fast-path's structural-marker condition is broken.
expectations: Either condition broken: structural markers present (H1, standfirst, byline, attributed quote).
`lib/genres/_index.md` is read and trigger-matched.
Genre commits to `article` and the corresponding `lib/genres/article.md` is read.
The default technique `abt` is loaded.

The findings list (`## Findings` in the transcript) includes at least one finding that cites a review-scoped section of `lib/genres/article.md` — specifically the repetition rule between H1, standfirst, lead and first H2, OR one of the documented common pitfalls (visible ABT formula, standfirst and lead repeating each other, teasing subheadings, explicit bridges). This is the observable consequence of the standard flow loading the genre file's review-scoped sections.

evals.name: max-iterations-natural-language-redline
evals.prompt: /redline sv en runda räcker på den här artikeln
evals.expected_output: Phase 3 delegation invokes the subagent with a ceiling of 1 (natural-language *en runda* → 1).
expectations: On delegation, the natural-language phrase *en runda räcker* is parsed to 1 — the same value as the `--max-iterations=1` flag.
The transcript states that on delegation, `lib/protocols/subagent.md` would be invoked with a ceiling of 1 — the parsed value of N from *en runda räcker*.

The transcript states the subagent ceiling N=1 only takes effect on delegation; without an explicit delegate-finding step the main agent applies the remaining open findings directly, no subagent invocation is recorded.
If the flag and the natural-language phrase conflict, the flag wins; if ambiguous, ask the user.

evals.name: max-iterations-natural-language-edit
evals.prompt: /edit sv max två rundor på den här artikeln
evals.expected_output: Phase 3 invokes the subagent with a ceiling of 2 (natural-language *max två rundor* → 2).
expectations: The natural-language phrase *max två rundor* is parsed to 2 — the same value as the `--max-iterations=2` flag.
The subagent loop in `lib/protocols/subagent.md` is invoked with a ceiling of 2.
The subagent's convergence rules still apply — early consensus stops the loop before 2 rounds.
Output is the polished text, with no user-facing dialogue (the AFK variant).

evals.name: max-iterations-natural-language-write
evals.prompt: /write skriv en krönika om varför svenska redaktioner ska ta AI på allvar — accept all, iterera max tre gånger i Phase 4
evals.expected_output: Phase 4 invokes the subagent with a ceiling of 3 (natural-language *iterera max tre gånger* → 3).
expectations: The natural-language phrase *iterera max tre gånger* is parsed to 3 — the same value as the `--max-iterations=3` flag.
The subagent loop in `lib/protocols/subagent.md` is invoked with a ceiling of 3.
Genre commits to `column` (default_technique `abt`) — the Swedish term *krönika* is in the trigger list.
There is no post-draft user-facing dialogue beyond the optional last-resort closing note.

evals.name: last-resort-floor-raises-to-one-when-flag-zero
evals.expected_output: Despite `--max-iterations=0`, the subagent floor is raised to 1 because the redline pass produces the last-resort developmental finding.
expectations: The redline pass identifies that the draft is below the line-editing repair threshold (vague claims, no concrete content, no hook, no source) and produces the last-resort finding from `lib/protocols/redline.md`.
The subagent floor is raised to 1 even though the flag is set to 0 — one round to sanity-check the observation.
The last-resort observation is delivered as a closing note to the user (e.g., *consider rewriting from richer source material*).
The main agent does not silently rewrite the developmental issues — those are escalated, not auto-fixed.

evals.name: phase-3-dialogue-mixed-accept-reject-counter
evals.prompt: /redline sv. Du presenterar fynden ett i taget. För denna körning scriptar jag mina svar i förväg: jag accepterar fynd 1 (svar: ”OK, kör”), avvisar fynd 2 (svar: ”nej, lämna det”), kontrar fynd 3 med en alternativ formulering du själv väljer som passar texten och som du tycker är substansmässigt minst lika bra som ditt original (svar: ”jag föreslår istället: <din alternativa formulering>”). För fynd 4 och framåt: accept på allt. Visa hela dialog-loopen i `## Phase log` med en rad per fynd och min scriptade respons.
evals.expected_output: Final polished text reflecting the scripted decisions: finding 1 applied, finding 2 dropped, finding 3 applied in the executor's counter-form, findings 4+ applied. Transcript Phase log records each branch.
expectations: `## Phase log` records Phase 3 entered with finding-by-finding presentation (one row per finding minimum).
Finding 1 is recorded as ACCEPTED in the Phase log; the proposed change appears in `output.md`.
Finding 2 is recorded as REJECTED in the Phase log; the proposed change does NOT appear in `output.md`.
Finding 3 is recorded as a COUNTER in the Phase log; the executor's alternative formulation is named in the transcript and that alternative (not the original proposal) appears in `output.md`.
Findings 4 and onward are recorded as ACCEPTED in the Phase log; those changes appear in `output.md`.
The without-skill baseline does not produce a structured Phase 3 record — the skill's value is the per-finding accountability.

evals.name: phase-3-dialogue-delegate-with-max-iterations
evals.prompt: /redline en_GB --max-iterations=2. You present the findings one at a time. My scripted responses for this run: I accept finding 1 ('OK'). I accept finding 2 ('yes, apply it'). After finding 2 I delegate the rest ('just do the rest, you decide'). Record the subagent ceiling N that `subagent.md` would be invoked with on the open tail — N comes from the `--max-iterations=2` flag, not natural language.
evals.expected_output: Findings 1 and 2 settled in dialogue (both accepted). The remaining open tail is delegated to subagent.md with N=2 from the flag. The polished text is delivered without a user-facing summary of internal work.
expectations: `## Phase log` records findings 1 and 2 as ACCEPTED via the dialogue protocol.
`## Phase log` records the user's delegation signal after finding 2 ('just do the rest' or close paraphrase).
`## Subagent ceiling` records N=2 and identifies the source as the `--max-iterations=2` flag (not a natural-language phrase, not the default 0, not the last-resort floor).

`## Phase log` notes that `lib/protocols/subagent.md` would be invoked on the open tail with N=2 (executor records what the subagent would do; no actual sub-subagent is spawned per the executor instructions).
`output.md` contains the polished text only — no user-facing summary of which findings were settled by the subagent.

evals.name: phase-3-dialogue-counter-defended
evals.prompt: /redline sv. Du presenterar fynden ett i taget. Scriptade svar: fynd 1 accept (”OK”). För fynd 2, om du föreslår ett mer publikvänligt språk: jag kontrar med argumentet att originalformuleringen är fackspråk som målgruppen (branschkollegor) förväntar sig (svar: ”nej, behåll original — fackspråk är vad målgruppen vill ha”). Du ska antingen försvara ditt ursprungliga förslag med en konkret hänvisning till en regel- eller genre-aspekt, eller acceptera mitt motargument och behålla originalet — och transkripten ska göra klart vilket. Resten av fynden: accept.
evals.expected_output: Finding 1 accepted. Finding 2 — the executor either defends with a concrete citation from a loaded rule/genre file, or adopts the user's counter and drops the proposal. The choice and its justification are explicit in the transcript. Remaining findings accepted.
expectations: Finding 1 is recorded as ACCEPTED in `## Phase log`.
The user's counter on finding 2 ('fackspråk är vad målgruppen vill ha' or close paraphrase) is quoted or summarised in the Phase log.

The executor's response on finding 2 is explicit: either (a) DEFENDS the original proposal with a concrete citation from a loaded file (a rule name + a one-line reason), or (b) ADOPTS the user's counter and drops the proposal. The transcript states which branch was taken.
If (a) defended: the original proposal appears in `output.md`. If (b) adopted: the original proposal does NOT appear in `output.md`.
Remaining findings (3+) are accepted in sequence; corresponding changes appear in `output.md`.
The transcript shows substantive engagement — no 'you're right, I agree' without analysis (sycophancy fail), no 'no, my proposal stands' without citation (stubborn fail).
